title: Transport Protocols, Ports, and Network Host Services · Fault-Isolation Clinic
summary: A realistic transport protocols, ports, and network host services ticket contains one plausible but causally wrong diagnosis or unsafe action. Find the first unsupported inference and repair the evidence chain. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in transport and service evidence map and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Contrast connection-oriented and connectionless transport, ports, sockets, sessions, reliability, ordering, flow, latency, and application responsibility.
Use current registries to recognize common service-port conventions while distinguishing registered, configured, observed, proxied, and tunneled behavior.
Trace web, mail, file, directory, remote-access, time, address, name, authentication, print, and database service dependencies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Bounded Service Test
steps.instruction:
Order the Bounded Service Test CompTIA A+ support HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Bounded Service Test.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Bounded Service Test assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For CompTIA A+ support Order the regression proof · Debug Bounded Service Test, keep Bounded Service Test connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Web content partly loads, mail sends but does not receive, and a firewall log uses familiar ports that do not prove the actual service. The debug variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Choose a safe name, address, route, transport, certificate, protocol, authentication, and application test with a predicted discriminating result.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Bounded Service Test has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Transport Behavior, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Transport Behavior record says transport and service evidence map looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Transport Behavior.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Bounded Service Test
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Bounded Service Test: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Web content partly loads, mail sends but does not receive, and a firewall log uses familiar ports that do not prove the actual service. The debug variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. steps.why: Retrieving Bounded Service Test through the concrete transport and service evidence map case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Bounded Service Test, keep Bounded Service Test connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose a safe name, address, route, transport, certificate, protocol, authentication, and application test with a predicted discriminating result.
